text: We have been performing a biliopancreatic diversion with the adenal switch using a hybrid laparoscopic robotic approach since two thousand. Recently we have modified a technique so that the entire procedure can be performed robotically with a single doc.

The surgeon identifies the ileocecal valve and places marking sutures at 100 centimeters and 250 centimeters from the valve. The bowel at the 250-centimeter mark is approximated to the anterior abdominal wall to facilitate the robotic procedure.

The surgeon dissects the duodenum, exposing at least four centimeters beyond the pylorus. The gastro duodenal vessel is identified, and a tunnel is created to accommodate the stapler for dividing the duodenum.

After dividing the duodenum, the surgeon mobilizes the greater curvature of the stomach up to the angle of His. A sleeve gastrectomy is performed using linear cutters, with a 34 French bougie in place to guide the resection.
The surgeon prepares the first part of the duodenum for anastomosis to the bowel at the 250-centimeter mark. A posterior layer is created using 3-0 absorbable sutures.

The surgeon opens the bowel using either a harmonic scalpel or hook cautery, initiating a full-thickness layer posteriorly. The suture is then continued anteriorly to complete the full-thickness layer.
The surgeon completes the anterior seromuscular layer and divides the bowel at the two hundred and fifty centimeter mark, separating the biliary limb from the duodenal ileal anastomosis.

The surgeon runs the alimentary limb until reaching the 100-centimeter mark, where enterotomies are made in both the alimentary and biliary limbs. A stapled anastomosis is performed to approximate the biliary limb to the common channel at this location.
The surgeon closes the enterotomy for the stapler using absorbable suture. Nonabsorbable suture is then used to approximate the mesentery of the common channel to the biliary channel.

The surgeon checks for leaks in the anastomosis using methylene blue or endoscopy, ensuring the integrity of the surgical connections. Specimens are removed from the surgical field, and the skin incisions are closed, completing the procedure.
